subject: Ecology
title: Yuccas and Yucca Moths
description: Learn Yuccas and Yucca Moths in one minute.

hook.text: Yucca moths deliberately pollinate yucca flowers—then lay eggs in the seeds they helped create.

buildup.text: The moth collects pollen into a ball and pushes it into the stigma—an intentional act.

discovery.text: Moth larvae eat some of the developing seeds, but enough survive to reproduce the plant.

twist.text: If the moth lays too many eggs, the plant aborts that fruit—punishing greedy partners.

climax.text: This balance between cooperation and cheating keeps the mutualism stable over millennia.

quiz.question: How do yucca plants prevent moth cheating?
options: They abort fruits with too many moth eggs
